prevalence.trend: increasing with obesity and metabolic syndrome

incidence.source: Primary care and lab cohort data

burden.dalyPerCapita: low direct burden; increases gout and CVD risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Elevated serum uric acid without gout symptoms; assess renal function and comorbidities

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening; detected via labs
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; treatment depends on risk profile

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Lab testing in primary care; evaluate secondary causes
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle modification; urate-lowering therapy reserved for gout or high-risk patients
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited lifestyle counseling and inconsistent follow-up
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable medications and lab access in many settings
